title: guard route-check for override spawns (D6)
action: Per D6: extend evaluateCodexSpawn — when tool_input carries any of model/reasoning_effort/fork_turns, validate against the configured route for the marker's tier/purpose: model must equal the configured native model, effort must equal configured, fork_turns must be 'none'; mismatch => deny with a named reason (e.g. codex-spawn-route-mismatch) quoting expected vs got. Spawns WITHOUT override fields keep today's behavior — existing test rows byte-unchanged (freeze green first per critical-patterns 20260716). A false DENY here locks bee out of its own dispatches: every new deny branch needs an allow-row twin proving the configured happy path. ADVISOR-R2 Δ3 (binding): the config read inside the route-check wraps in its own try/catch returning noOpinion() — audit/economics logging still runs; deny fires ONLY when the read succeeded AND override fields genuinely mismatch; a read error is an allow-hole by design (defense-in-depth), never a deny-storm. Freeze the no-override marker-only rows green BEFORE any edit. Δ4 CAP GATE: this cell must NOT be capped until V3 evidence exists (override fields observed in PreToolUse tool_input, from the live probe or cnt-5); V3 negative ⇒ rescope to document-the-gap + marker-only, and the cell record says so.

truths: existing no-override rows untouched and green before/after
every deny reason string names expected vs got
guard reads config through the same resolver as prepare (no duplicated route logic)
artifacts: allow+deny row pairs for each override field
prohibitions: no new deny path for spawns without override fields
no route logic duplicated outside the lib

trace.outcome: Rescoped per Δ4 negative branch (decision 350f1e82): cnt-5's probe evidence shows V3 terminal-UNOBSERVED on both probed codex builds (0.144.4 hook-never-fired, 0.144.6 API-level schema refusal), so cnt-4 ships document-the-gap + marker-only instead of the original D6 route-check. Added a bounded gap-documentation comment to evaluateCodexSpawn in skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/dispatch-guard.mjs (mirrored byte-identical to .bee/bin/lib/dispatch-guard.mjs) stating the route-check is intentionally absent pending V3 observation, citing probe-evidence.md and decision 350f1e82; no new deny branch was added — override fields (model/reasoning_effort/fork_turns) are still never read by evaluateCodexSpawn, so an override-carrying spawn passes through exactly like one without overrides, by design (ADVISOR-R2 Δ3 allow-hole). Added hooks/test_model_guard.mjs rows 56-57 proving the pass-through: row56 (anchored marker + deliberately mismatched override fields) is allowed; row57 (same override fields, unmarked message) stays denied, showing the marker rule is unaffected. Verified row56 is a real canary by temporarily injecting a stub route-check locally, observing row56 fail (status=2), then reverting and re-confirming green + mirror-identical. Existing no-override rows 1-55 stayed byte-unchanged and green throughout (frozen green before any edit).
trace.verify_output: hooks/test_model_guard.mjs: ALL PASS (57 rows incl. new rows 56-57 for the D6 pass-through-open canary). scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs: PASS x4 (self-test, runtime hook inventory, templates/lib<->bin/lib byte-identical 21 files, runtime-derived hook inventory byte-identical 10 files). Both commands exit 0.
